content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
James H. Marek, Jr. 
Auditor - Controller 
Napa County 
Administration 
•• 
Executive/legal 
322·5660 
322·5901 
June 8, 1983 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
1195 Third Street, Room B-10 
Napa, CA 
94559-3097 
Dear Mr. MareK: 
Re: 
Your Request for Written Advice 
Our No. A-83-138 
Your request for our advice relates that you have recently 
married and wiSh to know your reporting obligations for your 
wedding gifts when you next file your annual Statement of Economic 
Interests, Form 730 Schedule F (Gifts). 
You are a designated official under the Conflict of Interest 
Code for the County of Napa. 
Consequently, you must report only 
those gifts received from persons or businesses listed in the 
disclosure category which applies to you. 
As to those gifts, tney 
need be reportectll only if their value is $100 or more. 
In the 
case of wedding gifts, one-half of the value is attributed to your 
spouse (unless you received separate gifts). 
Under the provisions 
of Government COde Section 82028 (b) (3) 
(copy enclosed), you need 
not report gifts from certain relatives, regardless of their value. 
Shoulp you have any further questions, please do not hesitate 
to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
~~ncerely, 
) 
(-/ 
.~~ 
Robert E.J.eldigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
II Reporting requires that the name of the donor(s) be 
listed, together with their address, their business, the date of 
the gift, the gift's value (your one-half share) and a description 
of the gift. 


May 27. 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Office of Legal Counsel 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Dear Sir: 
I am writing you to seek a clarification concerning the reporting 
of my annual Statement of Economic Interest Form 730F, gifts. 
As you may know. I am the elected County Auditor of Napa County. 
On saturday April 3D, 1983 I ended my bachelorhood days and was 
married. 
Several dozen wedding presents were given to me and 
my wife by relatives. friends and employees of Napa County. 
My questions are: 
YOJ.r reply to 
JHM: 
1) 
Pursuant to Schedule 730F must I report 
my wedding presents to the FPPC on my 
next reporting date of April I, 1984? 
2) 
If so. are names, dates, dollar amounts 
and descriptions of wedding presents given 
me and my wife required to be reported 
only for gifts received that have a value 
of greater than $f(t'2 
3) 
Are all presents required to be reported 
regardless of value? 
4) 
Am I exempt from the reporting require-
ments of the Economic Interest Laws in 
regard to 
wedding 
? 
questions will 
anxiously awaited. 
James 
Mar

anxiously awaited. 
James 
Mar
sections.conclusion: You are a designated official under the Conflict of Interest Code for the County of Napa. Consequently, you must report only those gifts received from persons or businesses listed in the disclosure category which applies to you. As to those gifts, they need be reported only if their value is $100 or more. In the case of wedding gifts, one-half of the value is attributed to your spouse (unless you received separate gifts). Under the provisions of Government Code Section 82028 (b) (3), you need not report gifts from certain relatives, regardless of their value.
sections.question_synthetic: A designated official under the Napa County Conflict of Interest Code seeks clarification on whether wedding gifts must be reported on Schedule 730F (Gifts) of his Statement of Economic Interest, what threshold values apply, and whether any exemptions exist for such gifts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: As a designated official, the requestor must report wedding gifts valued at $100 or more from persons or businesses in his applicable disclosure category, with one-half of the gift value attributed to his spouse. Gifts from certain relatives are exempt from reporting under Government Code Section 82028(b)(3) regardless of value.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Complete advice letter with incoming request and FPPC response. OCR format with minor typos ('wiSh', 'reportectll', 'Shoulp'). Footnote included specifying required gift reporting details. Document number A-83-138.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: s will 
anxiously awaited. 
James 
Mar

CONCLUSION: You are a designated official under the Conflict of Interest Code for the County of Napa. Consequently, you must report only those gifts received from persons or businesses listed in the disclosure category which applies to you. As to those gifts, they need be reported only if their value is $100 or more. In the case of wedding gifts, one-half of the value is attributed to your spouse (unless you received separate gifts). Under the provisions of Government Code Section 82028 (b) (3), you need not report gifts from certain relatives, regardless of their value.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises James H. Marek, Jr., County Auditor of Napa, on his obligation to report wedding gifts on Schedule 730F, clarifying that only gifts of $100 or more from applicable disclosure sources must be reported, with one-half value attributed to his spouse, while certain relative gifts are entirely exempt.
